name: Tal Farlow
abstract: Talmage Holt Farlow (June 7, 1921 – July 25, 1998) was an American jazz guitarist. He was nicknamed "Octopus" because of how his large, quick hands spread over the fretboard.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Salute to Tal Farlow
Kool Jazz Festival: An Evening with Joe Williams and Friends

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: They would have loved Joe Pass doing that same thing. If Joe Pass went in and did that for 17 year olds they’d go crazy. Or Tal Farlow.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli, Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: he asked me about Tal Farlow, who was a dear friend... Very helpful. Gave me money when I needed it in L.A.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: This is the Gramercy Five with . . . Yeah, with Hank Jones, Tommy Potter, and Tal Farlow. That group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: who at that time was just a distant idol of mine who I knew that Tal Farlow had played with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Sal Salvador -- Tal Farlow, Jimmie Raney and myself had a thing; every night we met and we played all night long.

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: So Tal Farlow, he came in on a Sunday and sat in with us. Oh he played, oh what a good player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002
